Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Fixfest is a global gathering of repairers, tinkerers, and activists 81 upvotes, 3 comments
  2. Algebraic topology: knots links and braids 37 upvotes, 3 comments
  3. An opinionated take on how to do important research that matters 19 upvotes, 1 comments
  4. JSLinux Now Supports x86_64 69 upvotes, 12 comments
  5. Building a Procedural Hex Map with Wave Function Collapse 117 upvotes, 25 comments
  6. Fontcrafter: Turn Your Handwriting into a Real Font 346 upvotes, 114 comments
  7. What I Always Wanted to Know about Second Class Values 14 upvotes, 1 comments
  8. FreeBSD Capsicum vs. Linux Seccomp Process Sandboxing 82 upvotes, 27 comments
  9. Reverse-engineering the UniFi inform protocol 107 upvotes, 41 comments
  10. Segagaga Has Been Translated into English 83 upvotes, 32 comments
  11. Show HN: The Mog Programming Language 22 upvotes, 5 comments
  12. FFmpeg at Meta: Media Processing at Scale 172 upvotes, 78 comments
  13. Oil Is Near a Price That Hurts the Economy 28 upvotes, 8 comments
  14. Flash media longevity testing – 6 years later 82 upvotes, 34 comments
  15. Show HN: VS Code Agent Kanban: Task Management for the AI-Assisted Developer 78 upvotes, 36 comments
  16. Restoring a Sun SPARCstation IPX Part 1: PSU and Nvram 60 upvotes, 26 comments
  17. Ireland shuts last coal plant, becomes 15th coal-free country in Europe (2025) 682 upvotes, 414 comments
  18. The Window Chrome of Our Discontent 123 upvotes, 67 comments
  19. Jolla on track to ship new phone with Sailfish OS, user-replaceable battery 98 upvotes, 52 comments
  20. US Court of Appeals: TOS may be updated by email, use can imply consent [pdf] 475 upvotes, 369 comments
  21. Launch HN: Terminal Use (YC W26) – Vercel for filesystem-based agents 32 upvotes, 16 comments
  22. Unlocking Python's Cores:Energy Implications of Removing the GIL 99 upvotes, 73 comments
  23. The optimal age to freeze eggs is 19 28 upvotes, 17 comments
  24. Show HN: DenchClaw – Local CRM on Top of OpenClaw 37 upvotes, 27 comments
  25. Is legal the same as legitimate: AI reimplementation and the erosion of copyleft 129 upvotes, 128 comments
  26. Lazy JWT Key Rotation in .NET: Redis-Powered JWKS That Just Works 12 upvotes, 6 comments
  27. DARPA's new X-76 Experimental Plane 48 upvotes, 44 comments
